the diagram models the sequence of steps involved in authenticating a user within the unimove application.
it includes the flow of messages between the application, user management system, data management system, and firebase authentication api.
key edge cases include checking if the user is already logged in and handling scenarios where the user needs to log in or register.
the diagram highlights the use

sistema di gestione segnalazioni -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(getsegnalazioni())"; explanation="forward signals"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> database — type=message; label="query(select segnalazioni(*))"; explanation="query database for signals"
database -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="payload"; explanation="return payload"

this diagram models the process of reading signals in a system.
the application initiates the process by requesting signals from the signal management system.
the signal management system forwards the request to the data management system, which queries the database for signals.
the database returns the payload to the data management system, which then forwards it back to the signal management system.
finally, the signal management system displays either a null value or a list of signals to the application.
there is a conditional branch based on whether the list is null or not, leading to different display actions.

applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="setcalendario(cal)"; explanation="application sends calendar data to calendar management system"

sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(setcalendario(cal))"; explanation="calendar management system forwards calendar data to data management system"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> database — type=message; label="query(new calendario(cal))"; explanation="data management system queries database for new calendar data"
database -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="status code"; explanation="database returns status code to data management system"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(outcome)"; explanation="data management system forwards outcome to calendar management system"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="outcome"; explanation="calendar management system sends outcome to application"
applicazione -> applicazione — type=message; label="display(outcome)"; explanation="application displays outcome"
flow 1: applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — setcalendario(cal)
flow 2: sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — forward(setcalendario(cal))
flow 3: sistema di gestione dei dati -> database — query(new calendario(cal))
flow 4: database -> sistema di gestione dei dati — status code
flow 5: sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — forward(outcome)
flow 6: sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — outcome
flow 7: applicazione -> applicazione — display(outcome)
this sequence diagram models the insertion and modification process of a calendar within a system.
the application initiates the process by sending calendar data to the calendar management system.
the calendar management system then forwards this data to the data management system for processing.
the data management system queries the database for the new calendar data and returns a status code.
the data management system then forwards the outcome back to the calendar management system.
finally, the calendar management system displays the outcome to the application.
the process involves multiple components interacting through messages to ensure the calendar data is correctly managed and displayed.
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="calendario = getcalendario()"; explanation="get calendar data"
sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(getcalendario())"; explanation="forward calendar data"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> database — type=message; label="query(select calendario(*))"; explanation="query database for calendar data"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(payload)"; explanation="forward payload"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="calendario = payload"; explanation="set calendar data"
applicazione -> applicazione — type=message; label="[calendario == null] display(null)"; explanation="display null calendar"
applicazione -> applicazione — type=message; label="[calendario != null] display(calendario)"; explanation="display calendar data"

this diagram models the process of reading a calendar in a multi-component system.
the application initiates the process by requesting calendar data from the calendar management system.
the calendar management system forwards the request to the data management system, which queries the database for the calendar data.
the database returns the payload, which is then forwarded back to the calendar management system.
finally, the calendar management system sets the calendar data in the application, which displays it if available or null if not.
key edge cases include displaying null calendar data when no data is available.
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(gettrains(calendario))"; explanation="get trains from calendar"
sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(payload)"; explanation="forward payload"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> api trenitalia — type=message; label="requesttrains(calendario)"; explanation="request trains from trenitalia api"

sistema di gestione dei dati -> database — type=message; label="query(new treno(id, info))"; explanation="query database for new train"
database -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="status_code"; explanation="return status code"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(outcome)"; explanation="forward outcome"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="display(outcome)"; explanation="display outcome to application"

flow 2: sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — forward(payload)

flow 8: sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — display(outcome)
this diagram models the insertion and cancellation of preferred means in a transportation system.
the main flow starts with the application requesting a list of trains from the calendar management system.
the calendar management system forwards the request to the data management system, which then queries the trenitalia api for train information.
the data management system processes the response and queries the database to insert or update the train information.
the result is then forwarded back to the calendar management system and displayed to the application.
there are conditional branches for handling null lists of trains.
the system includes interactions with a database to store and retrieve train information.
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="list_trains = gettrains(calendario)"; explanation="get train list for calendar"
sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(gettrains(calendario))"; explanation="forward train list request"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> api trenitalia — type=message; label="requesttrains(calendario)"; explanation="request train list from trenitalia api"
api trenitalia -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="payload"; explanation="return payload with train list"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(payload)"; explanation="forward payload with train list"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="list_trains = payload"; explanation="set train list variable"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="[list_trains == null] display(null)"; explanation="display null list if train list is null"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="[list_trains != null] display_list(list_trains)"; explanation="display train list if not null"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="deletepreftrain(id)"; explanation="delete preferred train"

sistema gestione calendario -> api trenitalia — type=message; label="query(delete treno(id))"; explanation="query database to delete train"
api trenitalia -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="status code"; explanation="return status code for deletion"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="forward(outcome)"; explanation="forward outcome of deletion"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="display(outcome)"; explanation="display outcome to user"

applicazione -> sistema gestione variazioni — type=message; label="train_variations = getvariazioni()"; explanation="get variations"

this sequence diagram models the interaction between an application, a system for managing variations, and a notification system.
the application initiates the process by requesting variations from the variation management system.
the variation management system then starts the notification process.
finally, the notification system sends the notifications back to the application.
the diagram highlights the flow of data and control between the three systems, focusing on the notification process initiated by the variation management system.

sistema gestione calendario -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="forward(gettrains(calendario))"; explanation="forward train list request"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> api trenitalia — type=message; label="requesttrains(calendario)"; explanation="request train list from trenitalia api"
api trenitalia -> sistema di gestione dei dati — type=message; label="payload"; explanation="return payload with train list"
sistema di gestione dei dati -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(payload)"; explanation="forward payload with train list"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="list_trains = payload"; explanation="set train list variable"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="[list_trains == null] display(null)"; explanation="display null list if train list is null"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="[list_trains != null] display_list(list_trains)"; explanation="display train list if not null"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="deletepreftrain(id)"; explanation="delete preferred train"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="forward(deletepreftrain(id))"; explanation="forward delete train request"
sistema gestione calendario -> api trenitalia — type=message; label="query(delete treno(id))"; explanation="query database to delete train"
api trenitalia -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="status code"; explanation="return status code for deletion"
sistema gestione calendario -> applicazione — type=message; label="forward(outcome)"; explanation="forward outcome of deletion"
applicazione -> sistema gestione calendario — type=message; label="display(outcome)"; explanation="display outcome to user"

applicazione -> sistema gestione variazioni — type=message; label="train_variations = getvariazioni()"; explanation="get variations"
sistema gestione variazioni -> sistema di notifica — type=message; label="start()"; explanation="start notification process"
sistema di notifica -> applicazione — type=message; label="shownotifica(train_variations)"; explanation="show notifications"
flow 1: applicazione -> sistema gestione variazioni — "train_variations = getvariazioni()"
flow 2: sistema gestione variazioni -> sistema di notifica — "start()"
flow 3: sistema di notifica -> applicazione — "shownotifica(train_variations)"
this sequence diagram models the interaction between an application, a system for managing variations, and a notification system.
the application initiates the process by requesting variations from the variation management system.
the variation management system then starts the notification process.
finally, the notification system sends the notifications back to the application.
the diagram highlights the flow of data and control between the three systems, focusing on the notification process initiated by the variation management system.
